title: Regular expressions should avoid containing control characters
description: Regular expressions should not contain control charactersAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugregextype-dependentWhy is this an issue?More InfoEntries in the ASCII table below code 32 are known as control characters or non-printing characters. As they are not common in JavaScript strings, using these invisible characters in regular expressions is most likely a mistake.const pattern1 = /\x1a/; // Noncompliant: 1a (23 base 10) is less than 32 const pattern2 = new RegExp('\x1a'); // Noncompliant: 1a (23 base 10) is less than 32Instead, one should only match printable characters in regular expressions.const pattern1 = /\x20/; const pattern2 = new RegExp('\x20');Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud,
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
const pattern1 = /\x1a/;             // Noncompliant: 1a (23 base 10) is less than 32
const pattern2 = new RegExp('\x1a'); // Noncompliant: 1a (23 base 10) is less than 32
compliant:
const pattern1 = /\x20/;
const pattern2 = new RegExp('\x20');

checks.message: Regular expressions should avoid containing control characters (ASCII codes 0-31)
